ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that this is the 1st account, report and request for fees, instructions addressing the operation of the trust as a private foundation and wonders if this is really necessary. Mr. Der stated that it was done at the suggestion of Judge Sing, that the former trustee had issues, that they would like clarity going forward and that the trust has been treated as a private foundation for 13 years. The Court suggested that it may be better to stand on the existing order and proceed. Mr. Der stated that he is concerned as they go forward that there will be questions, that they are happy with the way the trust is being administered, that they fully understand how it works and that the former trustees counsel could not explain how it was being administered. The Court stated that there is no confusion or legal uncertainty regarding authority and that Judge Ertola got it right in 1984. Mr. Der stated that there is still some confusion and that an order confirming that the trust is being treated as a private foundation would be helpful. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Mitchell for Karen Anderson (Former Successor-Trustee).

Counsel explained circumstances, work performed, additional work required to address the Attorney General's concerns, time spent to address requests for additional information, etc. to support fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Bui. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Bond
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Off Calendar

12th And Final Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Approval, And Order Accepting Resignation Of Successor Trustee, Etc.
Off Calendar Dec-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Mitchell for and with Karen Anderson (current Successor-Trustee). Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (proposed Successor-Trustee). Scott Chan for The Attorney General's Office.

Mr. Chan stated that he has no objection to the resignation of Ms. Anderson and appointment of Ms. Dolch, and that the Attorney General takes no position with regard to the remaining issues. Ms. Mitchell stated that the issue of whether this Trust qualifies to be administered as a charitable distribution was addressed in 1999 and in 2000, and that the order signed by Judge Kay has findings regarding this issue. The Court clarified that the order included findings but that the language was not included in the order. Ms. Mitchell stated that this was not realized until the 11th accounting, that they may have to change something, that the accountings and the language were accepted by Court, that no charitable distribution was made in this accounting (12th accounting), that her Client will have a small supplemental accounting and that she believes they should leave it with the Successor Trustee as to whether they need to revise the Trust. Ms. Mitchell and Ms. Anderson presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Ms. Anderson's fees being paid at a rate of $200.00 per hour, explained services provided as well as Ms. Anderson's level of expertise, past practice of the Court to award Trustee fees. etc. The Court stated that Fiduciary fees are limited to $125.00 per hour, that Trustee fees may be reduced and that it would consider what was presented. The Court approved resignation of Ms. Anderson, the appointment of Ms. Dolch and granted the accounting petition (subject to further review of the Trustee fees). Bond in the amount of $875,000.00 is required. Status on filing of bond was set for January 6, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clark. (Not Reported)
